[doc://com.apple.scsicontrollerdriverkit/documentation/SCSIControllerDriverKit/IOUserSCSIParallelInterfaceController] supports this power state, in addition to the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/DriverKit/kIOServicePowerCapabilityOn] and [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/DriverKit/kIOServicePowerCapabilityOff] power states defined in the base [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/DriverKit] framework. # kIOServicePowerCapabilityPause

**Macro**

A PCIe-specific power state for halting transactions while reallocating resources.

## Declaration

```swift
#define kIOServicePowerCapabilityPause
```

## Discussion

[doc://com.apple.scsicontrollerdriverkit/documentation/SCSIControllerDriverKit/IOUserSCSIParallelInterfaceController] supports this power state, in addition to the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/DriverKit/kIOServicePowerCapabilityOn] and [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/DriverKit/kIOServicePowerCapabilityOff] power states defined in the base [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/DriverKit] framework. Implement the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/DriverKit/IOService/SetPowerState] method in your service object and use it to put your driver in a safe state for any of these power states.
title: kIOServicePowerCapabilityPause
